implementationGuidance: Define an Incident Response policy and procedures. Establish an Incident Response Team (IRT) with clear roles. Define incident classification criteria. Ensure procedures are tested and up to date. Assign an Incident Response Manager.
requiredDocuments: Incident Response Team (IRT) charter and contact list
bestPractices: Test the incident response plan at least annually with tabletop exercises
Maintain an on-call rota for the IRT
Align the IRP with NIST SP 800-61 or similar guidance

title: Reporting Information Security Events
description: Information security events shall be reported through appropriate management channels as quickly as possible.
purpose: To ensure security events are identified and reported so they can be assessed and responded to promptly.
implementationGuidance: Provide clear guidance to all staff on how and where to report suspected security events. Create easy-to-use reporting channels (email, portal, phone). Encourage reporting by fostering a no-blame culture. Acknowledge all reports and provide feedback.
evidenceExamples: Security event reporting channel details published to staff
Security awareness training covering event reporting
bestPractices: Make reporting easy — a single, memorable email address or button is ideal
Acknowledge every report with a status update
Recognise staff who report genuine security events

title: Reporting Information Security Weaknesses
description: Employees and contractors using the organisation's information systems and services shall be required to note and report any observed or suspected information security weaknesses in systems or services.
purpose: To enable the organisation to identify and address security weaknesses before they are exploited.
implementationGuidance: Train staff to recognise and report security weaknesses (vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, policy gaps). Provide a reporting mechanism. Investigate all reported weaknesses. Consider a responsible disclosure programme for external reports.
evidenceExamples: Received weakness reports and investigation records
bestPractices: Implement a formal responsible disclosure programme for external researchers
Track all reported weaknesses in an issue tracking system
Measure and report on time to resolve reported weaknesses

title: Assessment of and Decision on Information Security Events
description: Information security events shall be assessed and it shall be decided if they are to be classified as information security incidents.
purpose: To ensure security events are properly triaged and escalated to incident status when appropriate.
implementationGuidance: Define criteria for classifying events as incidents (e.g., based on impact, confidentiality, integrity, availability). Implement a triage process. Document assessment criteria. Assign responsibility for event assessment to qualified personnel.
evidenceExamples: Escalation records for events classified as incidents
bestPractices: Use a defined severity rating system (P1-P4 or Critical/High/Medium/Low)
Set maximum triage times by severity level
Review triage accuracy periodically to improve the process

title: Response to Information Security Incidents
description: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures.
purpose: To ensure incidents are contained, investigated, and resolved in a timely and structured manner.
implementationGuidance: Execute the incident response plan for confirmed incidents. Follow documented procedures for containment, eradication, and recovery. Maintain an incident log throughout. Communicate with stakeholders appropriately. Preserve evidence for potential legal or forensic use.
evidenceExamples: Incident response records for past incidents
bestPractices: Designate a single incident commander for major incidents
Use a dedicated incident channel (e.g., Slack war room) for real-time coordination
Document every action taken during an incident for post-incident review

title: Learning from Information Security Incidents
description: Knowledge gained from analysing and resolving information security incidents shall be used to reduce the likelihood or impact of future incidents.
purpose: To ensure incidents result in improvements to the organisation's security posture.
implementationGuidance: Conduct post-incident reviews (post-mortems) for all significant incidents. Document root cause, contributing factors, and lessons learned. Define and track action items to prevent recurrence. Share lessons learned with appropriate audiences.
evidenceExamples: Action item tracking records from post-mortems
bestPractices: Conduct blameless post-mortems focused on process improvement
Track all post-incident actions to closure
Include incident trend analysis in management reporting

title: Collection of Evidence
description: The organisation shall define and apply procedures for the identification, collection, acquisition and preservation of information, which can serve as evidence.
purpose: To ensure evidence collected during incident investigation is preserved in a way that supports legal proceedings if required.
implementationGuidance: Define evidence collection and preservation procedures before incidents occur. Train IRT members on forensically sound evidence collection. Maintain chain of custody records. Use forensic imaging tools for digital evidence. Consult legal counsel on evidence requirements.
evidenceExamples: Evidence collection training records for IRT members
Chain of custody records from past incidents
bestPractices: Use write-blocking tools when collecting digital evidence to prevent modification
Document all evidence collected with timestamps and chain of custody
Engage legal counsel early in incidents that may lead to prosecution or litigation

title: Planning Information Security Continuity
category: Information Security Aspects of Business Continuity Management
description: The organisation shall determine its requirements for information security and the continuity of information security management in adverse situations, e.g. during a crisis or disaster.
purpose: To ensure information security controls remain effective and available during disruptive events.
implementationGuidance: Include information security requirements in business continuity planning. Identify critical security controls that must be maintained during disruptions. Define minimum security requirements for reduced operational modes during a crisis.
requiredDocuments: Minimum security baseline for crisis operations
bestPractices: Integrate information security continuity into the organisation's overall BCM programme
Define RTO/RPO for security-critical systems
Test security control availability as part of BCP exercises

title: Implementing Information Security Continuity
category: Information Security Aspects of Business Continuity Management
description: The organisation shall establish, document, implement and maintain processes, procedures and controls to ensure the required level of continuity for information security during an adverse situation.
purpose: To ensure information security controls are implementable and operational during disruptions.
implementationGuidance: Develop and implement controls to maintain information security during disruptions. This includes redundant authentication systems, backup access control mechanisms, and secure communications for the crisis management team. Document all continuity controls.
bestPractices: Implement redundancy for critical security controls (e.g., dual SIEM, backup IAM)
Test continuity controls during BCP exercises
Ensure crisis team has out-of-band secure communications capability

title: Verify, Review and Evaluate Information Security Continuity
category: Information Security Aspects of Business Continuity Management
description: The organisation shall verify the established and implemented information security continuity controls at regular intervals in order to ensure that they are valid and effective during adverse situations.
purpose: To confirm that information security continuity controls will function as required during a real disruption.
implementationGuidance: Test information security continuity controls as part of BCP/DR exercises. Conduct annual tests of failover to backup security systems. Document test results and identify any gaps. Update continuity controls based on test outcomes.
evidenceExamples: BCP exercise records including security control testing
Failover test results for security systems
bestPractices: Include information security control failover in annual DR tests
Test the minimum security baseline under simulated crisis conditions
Document and close all gaps identified during continuity testing

title: Availability of Information Processing Facilities
category: Information Security Aspects of Business Continuity Management
description: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements.
purpose: To ensure business-critical information processing systems remain available even in the event of component failures.
implementationGuidance: Implement redundancy for critical information processing components (servers, network, storage). Define availability requirements (RTO/RPO) per system. Use high-availability architectures, clustering, and geographic redundancy for the most critical systems.
bestPractices: Design for N+1 redundancy as a minimum for all critical systems
Test failover regularly and under realistic load conditions
Monitor availability metrics against defined SLAs

title: Identification of Applicable Legislation and Contractual Requirements
description: All relevant legislative statutory, regulatory, contractual requirements and the organisation's approach to meet these requirements shall be explicitly identified, documented and kept up to date for each information system and the organisation.
purpose: To ensure the organisation understands its legal and contractual compliance obligations.
implementationGuidance: Maintain a legal and regulatory register identifying all applicable laws, regulations, and contractual requirements. Assign ownership for each requirement. Keep the register current by monitoring regulatory changes. Review the register at least annually.
evidenceExamples: Populated legal register with all applicable requirements
bestPractices: Assign a compliance owner responsible for monitoring regulatory changes
Subscribe to legal update services relevant to your sector and jurisdictions
Map legal requirements to ISMS controls to demonstrate compliance coverage

title: Intellectual Property Rights
description: Appropriate procedures shall be implemented to ensure compliance with legislative, regulatory and contractual requirements related to intellectual property rights and use of proprietary software products.
purpose: To protect the organisation from legal liability arising from unlicensed use of intellectual property.
implementationGuidance: Implement a software asset management programme to track all software licences. Conduct regular software audits to verify compliance. Prohibit installation of unlicensed software. Properly attribute and licence any open-source software used.
evidenceExamples: SAM tool reports showing licence compliance
Open-source software approval and attribution records
bestPractices: Use a SAM tool to automate licence tracking and compliance
Conduct annual software audits
Maintain an approved software catalogue with licence information

title: Protection of Records
description: Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorised access and unauthorised release, in accordance with legislatory, regulatory, contractual and business requirements.
purpose: To ensure important business records are maintained with appropriate protection throughout their lifecycle.
implementationGuidance: Define a records management programme covering record types, retention periods, storage requirements, access controls, and disposal procedures. Apply controls to protect records from loss, unauthorised access, and tampering.
evidenceExamples: Records retention schedule with legal basis
bestPractices: Align retention periods with legal requirements by record type and jurisdiction
Implement write-once storage for critical compliance records
Conduct periodic records management audits

title: Privacy and Protection of Personally Identifiable Information
description: Privacy and protection of personally identifiable information shall be ensured as required in relevant legislation and regulations where applicable.
purpose: To ensure the organisation processes personal data in compliance with applicable privacy laws.
implementationGuidance: Identify all personal data processed and applicable privacy laws (GDPR, DPDPA, CCPA, etc.). Implement controls to comply with each law's requirements. Appoint a Data Protection Officer if required. Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments for high-risk processing.
evidenceExamples: DPIA records for high-risk processing activities
bestPractices: Maintain a current and comprehensive Record of Processing Activities
Conduct DPIAs proactively for new high-risk processing activities
Align privacy controls across all applicable jurisdictions' requirements

title: Regulation of Cryptographic Controls
description: Cryptographic controls shall be used in compliance with all relevant agreements, laws and regulations.
purpose: To ensure the organisation's use of cryptography complies with local and international legal requirements.
implementationGuidance: Identify legal requirements regarding cryptography in all jurisdictions where the organisation operates (some countries restrict use of certain encryption strengths or algorithms). Review cryptographic implementations against these requirements. Consult legal counsel for cross-border data transfer encryption requirements.
requiredDocuments: Legal counsel advice on cryptographic controls
evidenceExamples: Legal review of cryptographic control compliance
Cryptography usage documentation with legal basis
Cross-border transfer mechanism documentation (e.g., SCCs, adequacy decisions)
bestPractices: Engage legal counsel to review cryptographic controls for all operating jurisdictions
Monitor changes to export control laws affecting cryptography
Document the legal basis for cryptographic choices in the ISMS

title: Independent Review of Information Security
description: The organisation's approach to managing information security and its implementation (i.e. control objectives, controls, policies, processes and procedures for information security) shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals or when significant changes occur.
purpose: To provide an objective assessment of the effectiveness of the ISMS.
implementationGuidance: Conduct periodic independent reviews of the ISMS by qualified internal auditors, external auditors, or third-party specialists. Document findings, recommendations, and management responses. Track and close findings within agreed timescales.
requiredDocuments: Audit reports and management response records
evidenceExamples: External or third-party security assessment reports
bestPractices: Conduct an annual internal audit of the full ISMS
Engage third-party specialists for independent assurance on high-risk areas
Track and report on finding closure rates to management

title: Compliance with Security Policies and Standards
description: Managers shall regularly review the compliance of information processing and procedures within their area of responsibility with the appropriate security policies, standards and any other security requirements.
purpose: To ensure ongoing compliance with security policies through regular managerial oversight.
implementationGuidance: Require managers to conduct regular reviews of security compliance within their areas. Implement compliance tracking and reporting mechanisms. Include security compliance in departmental performance reporting.
bestPractices: Provide managers with automated compliance status reports for their areas
Include security compliance in management performance objectives
Conduct risk-based compliance reviews focusing on highest risk areas

title: Technical Compliance Review
description: Information systems shall be regularly reviewed for compliance with the organisation's information security policies and standards.
purpose: To verify that technical security controls are operating as required by policy and standards.
implementationGuidance: Conduct regular technical compliance reviews using automated scanning tools, configuration management tools, and manual testing. Compare configurations against defined security baselines. Document findings and remediate non-compliant configurations within defined SLAs.
requiredDocuments: Technical compliance review schedule and reports
bestPractices: Automate configuration compliance checking using CIS Benchmarks or similar
Run compliance scans continuously or at minimum weekly
Report configuration compliance metrics to management monthly
